1: #!/usr/bin/env python3
2: """arXiv fetcher: search papers and download LaTeX source (.tar.gz).
3: 
4: NEVER downloads PDF. Only fetches metadata and LaTeX source archives.
5: """
6: 
7: import argparse
8: import json
9: import pathlib
10: import sys
11: import tarfile
12: import urllib.parse
13: import urllib.request
14: import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
15: 
16: NS = "http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom"
17: ARXIV_API = "http://export.arxiv.org/api/query"
18: SOURCE_DIR_DEFAULT = "papers/inbox"
19: 
20: 
21: def search(query, max_results=10):
22:     """Search arXiv API and return structured metadata."""
23:     encoded = urllib.parse.quote(query)
24:     url = (
25:         f"{ARXIV_API}?search_query={encoded}"
26:         f"&start=0&max_results={max_results}"
27:         f"&sortBy=relevance&sortOrder=descending"
28:     )
29:     with urllib.request.urlopen(url, timeout=30) as r:
30:         root = ET.fromstring(r.read())
31: 
32:     papers = []
33:     for entry in root.findall(f"{{{NS}}}entry"):
34:         aid = (
35:             entry.findtext(f"{{{NS}}}id", "")
36:             .split("/abs/")[-1]
37:             .split("v")[0]
38:         )
39:         title = (
40:             entry.findtext(f"{{{NS}}}title", "") or ""
41:         ).strip().replace("\n", " ")
42:         abstract = (
43:             entry.findtext(f"{{{NS}}}summary", "") or ""
44:         ).strip().replace("\n", " ")
45:         authors = [
46:             a.findtext(f"{{{NS}}}name", "")
47:             for a in entry.findall(f"{{{NS}}}author")
48:         ]
49:         published = entry.findtext(f"{{{NS}}}published", "")[:10]
50:         cats = [
51:             c.get("term", "")
52:             for c in entry.findall(f"{{{NS}}}category")
53:         ]
54: 
55:         papers.append({
56:             "id": aid,
57:             "title": title,
58:             "authors": authors,
59:             "abstract": abstract,
60:             "published": published,
61:             "categories": cats,
62:             "abs_url": f"https://arxiv.org/abs/{aid}",
63:             "source_url": f"https://arxiv.org/e-print/{aid}",
64:         })
65: 
66:     return papers
67: 
68: 
69: def download_source(arxiv_id, output_dir=SOURCE_DIR_DEFAULT):
70:     """Download LaTeX source (.tar.gz) and extract to output_dir/arxiv_id/."""
71:     out_path = pathlib.Path(output_dir) / arxiv_id
72:     source_url = f"https://arxiv.org/e-print/{arxiv_id}"
73: 
74:     if out_path.exists() and any(out_path.iterdir()):
75:         print(f"Already exists: {out_path}/")
76:         return str(out_path)
77: 
78:     out_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
79:     tar_path = out_path / "source.tar.gz"
80: 
81:     req = urllib.request.Request(source_url)
82:     req.add_header("User-Agent", "arxiv-skill/1.0")
83:     try:
84:         with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=60) as r:
85:             data = r.read()
86:     except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
87:         if e.code == 404:
88:             print(f"No LaTeX source available for {arxiv_id} (scanned/older paper)")
89:             try:
90:                 out_path.rmdir()
91:             except OSError:
92:                 pass
93:             return None
94:         raise
95: 
96:     if len(data) < 1024:
97:         print(f"Warning: source file suspiciously small ({len(data)} bytes)")
98:         try:
99:             out_path.rmdir()
100:         except OSError:
101:             pass
102:         return None
103: 
104:     tar_path.write_bytes(data)
105:     print(f"Downloaded: {tar_path} ({len(data) // 1024} KB)")
106: 
107:     # Extract
108:     try:
109:         with tarfile.open(tar_path, "r:gz") as tf:
110:             members = [
111:                 m for m in tf.getmembers()
112:                 if not m.name.startswith("/") and ".." not in m.name
113:             ]
114:             tf.extractall(path=out_path, members=members)
115:         print(f"Extracted to: {out_path}/")
116: 
117:         tex_files = list(out_path.rglob("*.tex"))
118:         if tex_files:
119:             print(f"Found {len(tex_files)} .tex file(s):")
120:             for f in sorted(tex_files):
121:                 rel = f.relative_to(out_path)
122:                 lines = f.read_text(errors="replace").count("\n")
123:                 print(f"  {rel} ({lines} lines)")
124:         else:
125:             print("No .tex files found — source may use different format")
126: 
127:     except tarfile.TarError:
128:         # Not gzip tar — might be single .tex file
129:         content = tar_path.read_bytes()
130:         try:
131:             text = content.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
132:             tex_path = out_path / "main.tex"
133:             tex_path.write_text(text)
134:             print(f"Saved as plain text: {tex_path}")
135:         except Exception:
136:             print("Could not extract source: unrecognized format")
137: 
138:     return str(out_path)
139: 
140: 
141: def fetch_paper(arxiv_id):
142:     """Fetch metadata for a single paper by ID."""
143:     url = f"{ARXIV_API}?id_list={arxiv_id}"
144:     with urllib.request.urlopen(url, timeout=30) as r:
145:         root = ET.fromstring(r.read())
146: 
147:     entries = root.findall(f"{{{NS}}}entry")
148:     if not entries:
149:         print(f"Paper not found: {arxiv_id}")
150:         return None
151: 
152:     entry = entries[0]
153:     aid = (
154:         entry.findtext(f"{{{NS}}}id", "")
155:         .split("/abs/")[-1]
156:         .split("v")[0]
157:     )
158:     title = (
159:         entry.findtext(f"{{{NS}}}title", "") or ""
160:     ).strip().replace("\n", " ")
161:     abstract = (
162:         entry.findtext(f"{{{NS}}}summary", "") or ""
163:     ).strip().replace("\n", " ")
164:     authors = [
165:         a.findtext(f"{{{NS}}}name", "")
166:         for a in entry.findall(f"{{{NS}}}author")
167:     ]
168:     published = entry.findtext(f"{{{NS}}}published", "")[:10]
169:     cats = [
170:         c.get("term", "")
171:         for c in entry.findall(f"{{{NS}}}category")
172:     ]
173: 
174:     return {
175:         "id": aid,
176:         "title": title,
177:         "authors": authors,
178:         "abstract": abstract,
179:         "published": published,
180:         "categories": cats,
181:         "abs_url": f"https://arxiv.org/abs/{aid}",
182:         "source_url": f"https://arxiv.org/e-print/{aid}",
183:     }
184: 
185: 
186: def main():
187:     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
188:         description="arXiv paper search and LaTeX source download"
189:     )
190:     sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command")
191: 
192:     s = sub.add_parser("search", help="Search arXiv")
193:     s.add_argument("query", help="Search query or 'id:ARXIV_ID'")
194:     s.add_argument("--max", type=int, default=10, dest="max_results")
195: 
196:     d = sub.add_parser("download", help="Download LaTeX source")
197:     d.add_argument("arxiv_id", help="arXiv ID (e.g. 2301.07041)")
198:     d.add_argument("--dir", default=SOURCE_DIR_DEFAULT, dest="output_dir")
199: 
200:     f = sub.add_parser("paper", help="Fetch single paper metadata")
201:     f.add_argument("arxiv_id", help="arXiv ID")
202: 
203:     args = parser.parse_args()
204: 
205:     if args.command == "search":
206:         results = search(args.query, args.max_results)
207:         print(json.dumps(results, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
208: 
209:     elif args.command == "download":
210:         result = download_source(args.arxiv_id, args.output_dir)
211:         if result is None:
212:             sys.exit(1)
213: 
214:     elif args.command == "paper":
215:         result = fetch_paper(args.arxiv_id)
216:         if result:
217:             print(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
218:         else:
219:             sys.exit(1)
220: 
221:     else:
222:         parser.print_help()
223: 
224: 
225: if __name__ == "__main__":
226:     main()

164: def compute_relevance(title, abstract):
165:     """Score paper relevance to LLM research.
166: 
167:     Uses per-tier hit capping to prevent keyword stacking.
168:     The score measures how centrally a paper is about LLMs,
169:     not how many LLM-related terms it happens to mention.
170:     """
171:     text = (title + " " + abstract).lower()
172: 
173:     # Title gets boosted — keywords in title indicate centrality
174:     title_lower = title.lower()
175: 
176:     # Collect positive scores with per-tier caps
177:     pos = 0.0
178:     for weight, keywords in INCLUDE.items():
179:         hits = 0
180:         cap = 1  # Only count 1 hit per tier (not stacking)
181:         for kw in keywords:
182:             if kw in text:
183:                 pos += weight
184:                 hits += 1
185:                 if hits >= cap:
186:                     break
187: 
188:     # Boost for core LLM keywords appearing in the title (strong signal)
189:     core_in_title = any(kw in title_lower for kw in INCLUDE.get(2, []))
190:     if core_in_title:
191:         pos += 1.5  # Title mention = the paper is ABOUT this topic
192: 
193:     # Extra boost for algorithm/architecture/data keywords in title
194:     # These are the user's primary interest areas
195:     ALGO_TITLE_KEYWORDS = [
196:         "pretrain", "architecture", "scaling law", "data mix",
197:         "mixture of expert", " moe ", "attention", "tokeniz",
198:         "fine-tun", "rlhf", "dpo", "rlvr", "alignment",
199:         "distillation", "pruning", "quantiz",
200:         "language model",
201:     ]
202:     if any(kw in title_lower for kw in ALGO_TITLE_KEYWORDS):
203:         pos += 1.0
204: 
205:     # Collect negative scores (no cap — exclusion should be strong)
206:     neg = 0.0
207:     for penalty, keywords in EXCLUDE.items():
208:         for kw in keywords:
209:             if kw in text:
210:                 neg += penalty
211: 
212:     # If no positive signal at all, this is not an LLM paper
213:     if pos == 0:
214:         return 0.0
215: 
216:     # Strong exclude: if negatives dominate, paper is not about LLMs
217:     if neg >= pos:
218:         return 0.0
219: 
220:     return pos - neg

223: def fetch_papers(categories, max_results, since_date):
224:     """Fetch papers from arXiv API."""
225:     cat_query = " OR ".join(f"cat:{c}" for c in categories)
226:     url = (
227:         f"{ARXIV_API}?search_query={urllib.parse.quote(cat_query)}"
228:         f"&sortBy=submittedDate&sortOrder=descending"
229:         f"&max_results={max_results}"
230:     )
231: 
232:     req = urllib.request.Request(url)
233:     req.add_header("User-Agent", "daily-papers-skill/1.0")
234: 
235:     try:
236:         with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=60, context=_SSL_CTX) as r:
237:             root = ET.fromstring(r.read())
238:     except Exception as e:
239:         print(f"Error fetching arXiv: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
240:         return []
241: 
242:     papers = []
243:     for entry in root.findall(f"{{{NS}}}entry"):
244:         aid = (
245:             entry.findtext(f"{{{NS}}}id", "")
246:             .split("/abs/")[-1]
247:             .split("v")[0]
248:         )
249:         title = (
250:             entry.findtext(f"{{{NS}}}title", "") or ""
251:         ).strip().replace("\n", " ")
252:         abstract = (
253:             entry.findtext(f"{{{NS}}}summary", "") or ""
254:         ).strip().replace("\n", " ")
255:         authors = [
256:             a.findtext(f"{{{NS}}}name", "")
257:             for a in entry.findall(f"{{{NS}}}author")
258:         ]
259:         published = entry.findtext(f"{{{NS}}}published", "")[:10]
260:         cats = [
261:             c.get("term", "")
262:             for c in entry.findall(f"{{{NS}}}category")
263:         ]
264: 
265:         # Date filter
266:         try:
267:             pub_date = datetime.strptime(published, "%Y-%m-%d").date()
268:         except ValueError:
269:             continue
270: 
271:         if pub_date < since_date:
272:             continue
273: 
274:         relevance = compute_relevance(title, abstract)
275: 
276:         papers.append({
277:             "id": aid,
278:             "title": title,
279:             "authors": authors,
280:             "abstract": abstract,
281:             "published": published,
282:             "categories": cats,
283:             "abs_url": f"https://arxiv.org/abs/{aid}",
284:             "source_url": f"https://arxiv.org/e-print/{aid}",
285:             "relevance_score": round(relevance, 1),
286:         })
287: 
288:     papers.sort(key=lambda p: p["relevance_score"], reverse=True)
289:     return papers

292: def _normalize_name(name):
293:     """Normalize author name for comparison: lowercase, strip accents, collapse whitespace."""
294:     import unicodedata
295:     name = unicodedata.normalize("NFKD", name.lower())
296:     name = "".join(c for c in name if not unicodedata.combining(c))
297:     return " ".join(name.split())

300: def check_author_openalex(author_name):
301:     """Look up author on OpenAlex (free, no API key). Returns dict or None.
302: 
303:     Uses display_name.search for exact-ish matching, then validates that
304:     the returned name is a reasonable match (not a completely different person).
305:     """
306:     params = urllib.parse.urlencode({
307:         "search": author_name,
308:         "per_page": 5,
309:         "select": "id,display_name,works_count,cited_by_count,summary_stats",
310:     })
311:     url = f"{OPENALEX_API}/authors?{params}"
312: 
313:     req = urllib.request.Request(url)
314:     req.add_header("User-Agent", "mailto:daily-papers-skill@example.com")
315: 
316:     try:
317:         with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15, context=_SSL_CTX) as r:
318:             data = json.loads(r.read())
319:     except Exception:
320:         return None
321: 
322:     results = data.get("results") or []
323:     if not results:
324:         return None
325: 
326:     # Pick the best match by name similarity
327:     target = _normalize_name(author_name)
328:     best = None
329:     best_score = 0.0
330: 
331:     for a in results:
332:         candidate = _normalize_name(a.get("display_name", ""))
333:         # Exact match
334:         if candidate == target:
335:             best = a
336:             break
337:         # Last name match (most discriminative for Chinese/Korean names)
338:         target_parts = target.split()
339:         candidate_parts = candidate.split()
340:         if target_parts and candidate_parts and target_parts[-1] == candidate_parts[-1]:
341:             score = 0.7
342:             # First initial match bonus
343:             if target_parts[0][0] == candidate_parts[0][0]:
344:                 score = 0.9
345:             if score > best_score:
346:                 best_score = score
347:                 best = a
348: 
349:     if not best:
350:         # Fallback to first result but mark as uncertain
351:         best = results[0]
352: 
353:     stats = best.get("summary_stats") or {}
354:     return {
355:         "name": best.get("display_name"),
356:         "paperCount": best.get("works_count", 0),
357:         "citationCount": best.get("cited_by_count", 0),
358:         "hIndex": stats.get("h_index", 0),
359:         "i10Index": stats.get("i10_index", 0),
360:         "match_confidence": "exact" if best is results[0] and _normalize_name(results[0].get("display_name", "")) == target else "approximate",
361:     }

364: def check_paper_openalex(arxiv_id):
365:     """Look up paper on OpenAlex by arXiv ID. Returns authorships or None."""
366:     # OpenAlex uses doi format: 10.48550/arXiv.XXXX.XXXXX
367:     doi = f"10.48550/arXiv.{arxiv_id}"
368:     url = f"{OPENALEX_API}/works/doi:{urllib.parse.quote(doi)}?select=authorships,cited_by_count"
369:

372: 
373:     try:
374:         with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15, context=_SSL_CTX) as r:
375:             data = json.loads(r.read())
376:     except Exception:
377:         return None

382: def _classify_institution(institution_name):
383:     """Classify institution into tier1/tier2/unknown."""
384:     if not institution_name or institution_name == "unknown":
385:         return "unknown"
386:     inst_lower = institution_name.lower()
387:     for pattern in TIER1:
388:         if pattern in inst_lower:
389:             return "tier1"
390:     for pattern in TIER2:
391:         if pattern in inst_lower:
392:             return "tier2"
393:     return "unknown"

396: def enrich_with_authors(papers):
397:     """Add OpenAlex author quality + institution data. Free, no API key."""
398:     for i, paper in enumerate(papers):
399:         if not paper["authors"]:
400:             paper["author_quality"] = "unknown"
401:             paper["first_author_institution"] = "unknown"
402:             paper["first_author_institution_tier"] = "unknown"
403:             paper["first_author_openalex"] = None
404:             paper["paper_citations_openalex"] = None
405:             continue
406: 
407:         first_author = paper["authors"][0]
408: 
409:         # Try paper-level lookup first (most accurate for institutions)
410:         oa_paper = check_paper_openalex(paper["id"])
411:         institution = "unknown"
412:         inst_tier = "unknown"
413:         paper_citations = None
414: 
415:         if oa_paper:
416:             paper_citations = oa_paper.get("cited_by_count")
417:             authorships = oa_paper.get("authorships") or []
418:             for a in authorships:
419:                 oa_name = _normalize_name(
420:                     a.get("author", {}).get("display_name", "")
421:                 )
422:                 if oa_name == _normalize_name(first_author):
423:                     insts = a.get("institutions") or []
424:                     if insts:
425:                         institution = insts[0].get("display_name", "unknown")
426:                         inst_tier = _classify_institution(institution)
427:                     # Also get author OpenAlex ID for more reliable profile lookup
428:                     oa_author_id = a.get("author", {}).get("id")
429:                     if oa_author_id:
430:                         # Fetch author profile by ID (exact match)
431:                         try:
432:                             req = urllib.request.Request(oa_author_id)
433:                             req.add_header("User-Agent", "mailto:daily-papers-skill@example.com")
434:                             with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15, context=_SSL_CTX) as r:
435:                                 adata = json.loads(r.read())
436:                             stats = adata.get("summary_stats") or {}
437:                             oa_author = {
438:                                 "name": adata.get("display_name"),
439:                                 "paperCount": adata.get("works_count", 0),
440:                                 "citationCount": adata.get("cited_by_count", 0),
441:                                 "hIndex": stats.get("h_index", 0),
442:                                 "i10Index": stats.get("i10_index", 0),
443:                                 "match_confidence": "exact",
444:                             }
445:                             paper["first_author_openalex"] = oa_author
446:                         except Exception:
447:                             pass
448:                     break
449: 
450:         # Fallback: author profile lookup by name search
451:         if paper.get("first_author_openalex") is None:
452:             oa_author = check_author_openalex(first_author)
453:             paper["first_author_openalex"] = oa_author
454: 
455:         paper["first_author_institution"] = institution
456:         paper["first_author_institution_tier"] = inst_tier
457:         paper["paper_citations_openalex"] = paper_citations
458: 
459:         oa_author = paper.get("first_author_openalex")
460:         if oa_author:
461:             h = oa_author.get("hIndex", 0) or 0
462:             c = oa_author.get("citationCount", 0) or 0
463:             confidence = oa_author.get("match_confidence", "approximate")
464: 
465:             # If match is approximate, be more conservative
466:             if confidence == "approximate":
467:                 h = h * 0.5
468:                 c = c * 0.5
469: 
470:             if h >= 20 or c >= 5000:
471:                 paper["author_quality"] = "high"
472:             elif h >= 10 or c >= 1000:
473:                 paper["author_quality"] = "medium"
474:             elif c >= 100:
475:                 paper["author_quality"] = "low"
476:             else:
477:                 paper["author_quality"] = "unknown"
478:         else:
479:             paper["author_quality"] = "unknown"
480: 
481:         # OpenAlex is less strict on rate limits than S2, but still be nice
482:         if i < len(papers) - 1:
483:             time.sleep(0.3)
484: 
485:     return papers
486:

488: def main():
489:     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
490:         description="Daily LLM Papers: fetch and filter from arXiv"
491:     )
492:     parser.add_argument(
493:         "command",
494:         choices=["fetch", "run"],
495:         help="fetch=raw papers, run=fetch+filter+score",
496:     )
497:     parser.add_argument(
498:         "--date", default=None,
499:         help="Date YYYY-MM-DD (default: today)",
500:     )
501:     parser.add_argument(
502:         "--categories", default="cs.CL,cs.AI,cs.LG",
503:         help="arXiv categories (comma-separated)",
504:     )
505:     parser.add_argument(
506:         "--max", type=int, default=150,
507:         dest="max_results",
508:         help="Max papers to fetch from arXiv",
509:     )
510:     parser.add_argument(
511:         "--min-score", type=float, default=0.5,
512:         help="Minimum relevance score to include",
513:     )
514:     parser.add_argument(
515:         "--check-authors", action="store_true",
516:         help="Look up first author on OpenAlex (free, no API key needed)",
517:     )
518: 
519:     args = parser.parse_args()
520: 
521:     if args.date:
522:         since = datetime.strptime(args.date, "%Y-%m-%d").date()
523:     else:
524:         since = datetime.now().date() - timedelta(days=1)
525: 
526:     categories = [c.strip() for c in args.categories.split(",")]
527: 
528:     papers = fetch_papers(categories, args.max_results, since)
529: 
530:     if args.command == "fetch":
531:         print(json.dumps(papers, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
532:         return
533: 
534:     # Run: filter + score
535:     filtered = [p for p in papers if p["relevance_score"] >= args.min_score]
536: 
537:     if args.check_authors:
538:         filtered = enrich_with_authors(filtered)
539: 
540:     # Tier assignment
541:     for p in filtered:

544:             p["tier"] = "MUST_READ"
545:         elif score >= 2.5:

547:         elif score >= 1:
548:             p["tier"] = "MARGINAL"
549:         else:
550:             p["tier"] = "SKIP"
551: 
552:     output = {

554:         "categories": categories,
555:         "total_fetched": len(papers),
556:         "total_filtered": len(filtered),

558:     }

1: #!/usr/bin/env python3
2: """PDF parser: extract structured content from local academic PDFs using docling.
3: 
4: Converts PDF to Markdown with section structure and extracts metadata.
5: No Docker or external services required — runs entirely locally.
6: """
7: 
8: import argparse
9: import json
10: import pathlib
11: import re
12: import sys
13: 
14: SOURCE_DIR_DEFAULT = "papers/inbox"
15: MAX_PDF_SIZE = 100 * 1024 * 1024  # 100 MB
16: 
17: 
18: def _generate_slug(metadata: dict, pdf_path: pathlib.Path) -> str:
19:     """Generate a slug from the paper title or filename."""
20:     title = metadata.get("title", "")
21:     if title:
22:         slug = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", title.lower()).strip("-")
23:         slug = re.sub(r"-+", "-", slug)
24:         return slug[:60]
25: 
26:     stem = pdf_path.stem.lower()
27:     slug = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", stem).strip("-")
28:     return slug[:60]
29:

31: def _extract_metadata(result) -> dict:
32:     """Extract metadata from docling DocumentConverter result."""
33:     metadata = {"source_type": "pdf"}
34: 
35:     doc = result.document
36:     if doc is None:
37:         return metadata
38: 
39:     # Title from document name
40:     name = getattr(doc, "name", None)
41:     if name:
42:         metadata["title"] = str(name)
43: 
44:     # Try to extract from docling's metadata
45:     doc_meta = getattr(doc, "metadata", None)
46:     if doc_meta:
47:         if hasattr(doc_meta, "title") and doc_meta.title:
48:             metadata["title"] = doc_meta.title
49:         if hasattr(doc_meta, "authors") and doc_meta.authors:
50:             metadata["authors"] = [
51:                 {"full": str(a)} for a in doc_meta.authors
52:             ]
53:         if hasattr(doc_meta, "date") and doc_meta.date:
54:             metadata["date"] = str(doc_meta.date)
55: 
56:     return metadata

59: def _extract_arxiv_id(text: str) -> str | None:
60:     """Detect arXiv ID in text (filename, title, etc.)."""
61:     # New format: YYMM.NNNNN
62:     m = re.search(r"(?<!\d)(\d{4}\.\d{4,5})(?!\d)", text)
63:     if m:
64:         return m.group(1)
65:     # Old format: category/NNNNNNN
66:     m = re.search(r"([a-z-]+/\d{7})", text)
67:     if m:
68:         return m.group(1)
69:     return None

72: def parse(
73:     pdf_path: str,
74:     output_dir: str = SOURCE_DIR_DEFAULT,
75:     ocr: bool = True,
76:     formula: bool = False,
77: ) -> dict:
78:     """Parse a single PDF using docling, convert to Markdown, extract metadata."""
79:     from docling.backend.docling_parse_backend import DoclingParseDocumentBackend
80:     from docling.datamodel.base_models import InputFormat
81:     from docling.datamodel.pipeline_options import PdfPipelineOptions
82:     from docling.document_converter import DocumentConverter, FormatOption
83:     from docling.pipeline.standard_pdf_pipeline import StandardPdfPipeline
84: 
85:     path = pathlib.Path(pdf_path)
86: 
87:     # Validate file
88:     if not path.exists():
89:         print(f"Error: file not found: {pdf_path}", file=sys.stderr)
90:         sys.exit(1)
91: 
92:     if not path.is_file():
93:         print(f"Error: not a file: {pdf_path}", file=sys.stderr)
94:         sys.exit(1)
95: 
96:     # Check magic bytes
97:     header = path.read_bytes()[:5]
98:     if header != b"%PDF-":
99:         print(f"Warning: file may not be a valid PDF (magic bytes: {header!r})")
100: 
101:     # Check size
102:     size = path.stat().st_size
103:     if size > MAX_PDF_SIZE:
104:         print(f"Warning: file is large ({size // (1024*1024)} MB), parsing may be slow")
105: 
106:     # Configure pipeline options
107:     pipeline_options = PdfPipelineOptions(
108:         do_ocr=ocr,
109:         do_table_structure=True,
110:         do_formula_enrichment=formula,
111:         document_timeout=180.0,
112:     )
113: 
114:     # Parse with docling
115:     print(f"Parsing {path.name} ({size // 1024} KB) with docling...")
116:     converter = DocumentConverter(
117:         format_options={
118:             InputFormat.PDF: FormatOption(
119:                 pipeline_options=pipeline_options,
120:                 backend=DoclingParseDocumentBackend,
121:                 pipeline_cls=StandardPdfPipeline,
122:             ),
123:         }
124:     )
125:     result = converter.convert(str(path))
126: 
127:     # Export to Markdown
128:     markdown = result.document.export_to_markdown()
129: 
130:     # Post-process: remove page number artifacts (bare numbers on their own lines)
131:     lines = markdown.split("\n")
132:     cleaned = []
133:     for line in lines:
134:         stripped = line.strip()
135:         # Skip lines that are just a number (page number artifact)
136:         if re.match(r"^\d{1,4}$", stripped):
137:             continue
138:         cleaned.append(line)
139:     markdown = "\n".join(cleaned)
140: 
141:     # Collapse multiple blank lines into at most two
142:     markdown = re.sub(r"\n{3,}", "\n\n", markdown)
143: 
144:     # Extract metadata
145:     metadata = _extract_metadata(result)
146: 
147:     # Try to extract title from first heading in Markdown if not in metadata
148:     if not metadata.get("title") or metadata.get("title", "").startswith(path.stem[:10]):
149:         lines = markdown.split("\n")
150:         for line in lines:
151:             stripped = line.strip()
152:             if stripped.startswith("## ") and not stripped.lower().startswith("## abstract"):
153:                 # First real section heading is often the title
154:                 candidate = stripped[3:].strip()
155:                 if len(candidate) > 10 and len(candidate) < 200:
156:                     metadata["title"] = candidate
157:                     break
158:         # Fallback: use first ## heading of any kind
159:         if not metadata.get("title") or metadata.get("title", "").startswith(path.stem[:10]):
160:             for line in lines:
161:                 stripped = line.strip()
162:                 if stripped.startswith("#"):
163:                     candidate = stripped.lstrip("#").strip()
164:                     if len(candidate) > 5:
165:                         metadata["title"] = candidate
166:                         break
167: 
168:     # Detect arXiv ID
169:     arxiv_id = _extract_arxiv_id(path.name)
170:     if not arxiv_id and metadata.get("title"):
171:         arxiv_id = _extract_arxiv_id(metadata["title"])
172:     if arxiv_id:
173:         metadata["arxiv_id"] = arxiv_id
174: 
175:     # Generate slug
176:     slug = _generate_slug(metadata, path)
177:     out_path = pathlib.Path(output_dir) / slug
178:     out_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
179: 
180:     # Save Markdown
181:     md_path = out_path / "paper.md"
182:     md_path.write_text(markdown, errors="replace")
183: 
184:     # Save metadata
185:     meta_path = out_path / "metadata.json"
186:     meta_path.write_text(
187:         json.dumps(metadata, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
188:     )
189: 
190:     # Count sections (lines starting with ##)
191:     section_count = sum(
192:         1 for line in markdown.split("\n")
193:         if line.strip().startswith("## ")
194:     )
195: 
196:     print(f"Parsed: {out_path}/")
197:     print(f"  Title: {metadata.get('title', 'N/A')}")
198:     print(f"  Authors: {len(metadata.get('authors', []))}")
199:     print(f"  Sections: {section_count}")
200:     print(f"  Files: paper.md, metadata.json")
201: 
202:     return {
203:         "slug": slug,
204:         "output_dir": str(out_path),
205:         "markdown_file": str(md_path),

1: ---
2: name: arxiv
3: description: Search arXiv and download LaTeX source for academic papers. Use when user says "search arxiv", "download paper", "fetch arxiv", "arxiv search", "get paper source", or wants to find and read papers from arXiv. PDF download is BANNED — only LaTeX source (.tar.gz) is allowed.
4: argument-hint: [query-or-arxiv-id]
5: allowed-tools: Bash(*), Read, Grep, Glob, Write
6: ---
7: 
8: # arXiv Paper Search & LaTeX Source Download
9: 
10: Search topic or arXiv paper ID: $ARGUMENTS
11: 
12: > **CRITICAL RULE: PDF is BANNED.** Never download, fetch, or link to PDF files.
13: > Only download LaTeX source archives from `https://arxiv.org/e-print/ID`.
14: > LaTeX source can be searched with Grep, read section-by-section with Read,
15: > and analyzed far more effectively than PDF.
16: 
17: ## Constants
18: 
19: - **PAPER_DIR** - Local directory to save downloaded LaTeX source. Default: `papers/inbox/` in the current project directory (dual-zone: inbox for new papers, archive for synthesized papers).
20: - **MAX_RESULTS = 10** - Default number of search results.
21: - **FETCH_SCRIPT** - `tools/arxiv_fetch.py` relative to the project root. Fall back to inline Python if not found.
22: 
23: > Overrides (append to arguments):
24: > - `/arxiv "attention mechanism" - max: 20` - return up to 20 results
25: > - `/arxiv "2301.07041" - download` - download LaTeX source for a specific paper by ID
26: > - `/arxiv "query" - dir: literature/` - save source to a custom directory
27: > - `/arxiv "query" - download: all` - download source for all results
28: 
29: ## Workflow
30: 
31: ### Step 1: Parse Arguments
32: 
33: Parse `$ARGUMENTS` for directives:
34: 
35: - **Query or ID**: main search term or a bare arXiv ID such as `2301.07041` or `cs/0601001`
36: - **`- max: N`**: override MAX_RESULTS (e.g., `- max: 20`)
37: - **`- dir: PATH`**: override PAPER_DIR (e.g., `- dir: literature/`)
38: - **`- download`**: download the first result's LaTeX source after listing
39: - **`- download: all`**: download LaTeX source for all results
40: 
41: If the argument matches an arXiv ID pattern (`YYMM.NNNNN` or `category/NNNNNNN`), skip the search and go directly to Step 3.
42: 
43: ### Step 2: Search arXiv
44: 
45: Locate the fetch script:
46: 
47: ```bash
48: SCRIPT=$(find tools/ -name "arxiv_fetch.py" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
49: ```
50: 
51: **If SCRIPT is found**, run:
52: 
53: ```bash
54: python3 "$SCRIPT" search "QUERY" --max MAX_RESULTS
55: ```
56: 
57: **If SCRIPT is not found**, fall back to inline Python:
58: 
59: ```bash
60: python3 - <<'PYEOF'
61: import json
62: import urllib.parse
63: import urllib.request
64: import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
65: 
66: NS = "http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom"
67: query = urllib.parse.quote("QUERY")
68: url = (f"http://export.arxiv.org/api/query"
69:        f"?search_query={query}&start=0&max_results=MAX_RESULTS"
70:        f"&sortBy=relevance&sortOrder=descending")
71: with urllib.request.urlopen(url, timeout=30) as r:
72:     root = ET.fromstring(r.read())
73: papers = []
74: for entry in root.findall(f"{{{NS}}}entry"):
75:     aid = entry.findtext(f"{{{NS}}}id", "").split("/abs/")[-1].split("v")[0]
76:     title = (entry.findtext(f"{{{NS}}}title", "") or "").strip().replace("\n", " ")
77:     abstract = (entry.findtext(f"{{{NS}}}summary", "") or "").strip().replace("\n", " ")
78:     authors = [a.findtext(f"{{{NS}}}name", "") for a in entry.findall(f"{{{NS}}}author")]
79:     published = entry.findtext(f"{{{NS}}}published", "")[:10]
80:     cats = [c.get("term", "") for c in entry.findall(f"{{{NS}}}category")]
81:     papers.append({
82:         "id": aid,
83:         "title": title,
84:         "authors": authors,
85:         "abstract": abstract,
86:         "published": published,
87:         "categories": cats,
88:         "abs_url": f"https://arxiv.org/abs/{aid}",
89:         "source_url": f"https://arxiv.org/e-print/{aid}",
90:     })
91: print(json.dumps(papers, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
92: PYEOF
93: ```
94: 
95: Present results as a table:
96: 
97: ```text
98: | # | arXiv ID   | Title               | Authors        | Date       | Category |
99: |---|------------|---------------------|----------------|------------|----------|
100: | 1 | 2301.07041 | Attention Is All... | Vaswani et al. | 2017-06-12 | cs.LG    |
101: ```
102: 
103: ### Step 3: Fetch Details for a Specific ID
104: 
105: When a single paper ID is requested (either directly or from Step 2):
106: 
107: ```bash
108: python3 "$SCRIPT" paper "ARXIV_ID"
109: ```
110: 
111: Display: title, all authors, categories, full abstract, published date, abstract URL, source URL.
112: 
113: ### Step 4: Download LaTeX Source
114: 
115: When download is requested, for each paper ID to download:
116: 
117: ```bash
118: # Using fetch script:
119: python3 "$SCRIPT" download ARXIV_ID --dir PAPER_DIR
120: ```
121: 
122: The script will:
123: 1. Download `https://arxiv.org/e-print/ARXIV_ID` (LaTeX source archive)
124: 2. Extract to `PAPER_DIR/ARXIV_ID/`
125: 3. List all `.tex` files found with line counts
126: 
127: After each download:
128: 
129: - Confirm the extracted directory contains `.tex` files
130: - If no LaTeX source is available (404 — scanned/older paper), report it clearly
131: - Add a 1-second delay between consecutive downloads to avoid rate limiting
132: - Report: `Downloaded: papers/inbox/2301.07041/ (N .tex files)`
133: 
134: **Once downloaded, read the paper using Read and Grep on the .tex files:**
135: 
136: ```bash
137: # Find the main .tex file (usually main.tex, ms.tex, or paper.tex)
138: # Read the full paper
139: Read: papers/inbox/2301.07041/main.tex
140: 
141: # Search for specific content
142: Grep: "attention" in papers/inbox/2301.07041/
143: 
144: # Read a specific section
145: Read: papers/inbox/2301.07041/experiments.tex
146: ```
147: 
148: ### Step 5: Summarize
149: 
150: For each paper (downloaded or fetched by API):
151: 
152: ```markdown
153: ## [Title]
154: 
155: - **arXiv**: [ID] - [abs_url]
156: - **Authors**: [full author list]
157: - **Date**: [published]
158: - **Categories**: [cs.LG, cs.AI, ...]
159: - **Abstract**: [full abstract]
160: - **Key contributions** (extracted from abstract):
161:   - [contribution 1]
162:   - [contribution 2]
163:   - [contribution 3]
164: - **LaTeX source**: papers/inbox/[ID]/ (if downloaded — read .tex files for full content)
165: ```
166: 
167: If LaTeX source was downloaded, additionally extract from the .tex files:
168: - Methodology details (from method/approach sections)
169: - Key equations (search for `\begin{equation}`, `\begin{align}`)
170: - Experimental setup (from experiments section)
171: - Results tables (search for `\begin{table}`)
172: 
173: ### Step 6: Final Output
174: 
175: Summarize what was done:
176: 
177: - `Found N papers for "query"`
178: - `Downloaded source: papers/inbox/2301.07041/ (N .tex files)` (for each download)
179: - Any warnings (rate limit hit, no LaTeX source available, already exists)
180: 
181: Suggest follow-up skills:
182: 
183: ```text
184: /read-paper ARXIV_ID      - deep-read this paper from LaTeX source
185: /search-knowledge "topic"  - search across all read paper notes
186: ```
187: 
188: ## Key Rules
189: 
190: - **PDF IS BANNED.** Never download, link to, or suggest PDF files. Only use LaTeX source from `https://arxiv.org/e-print/ID`.
191: - Always show the arXiv ID prominently - users need it for citations and reproducibility
192: - Verify downloaded source: directory must contain at least one `.tex` file
193: - Rate limit: wait 1 second between consecutive source downloads; retry once after 5 seconds on HTTP 429
194: - Never overwrite an existing source directory - skip it and report "already exists"
195: - Handle both arXiv ID formats: new (`2301.07041`) and old (`cs/0601001`)
196: - PAPER_DIR is created automatically if it does not exist
197: - If a paper has no LaTeX source (scanned/older papers), report this clearly — this paper cannot be read with `/read-paper`
198: - If the arXiv API is unreachable, report the error clearly and suggest trying again later
199: - When LaTeX source is available, always prefer reading .tex files over abstract-only summaries

(End of file - total 199 lines)
</content>

1: ---
2: name: search-knowledge
3: description: Search across all read paper notes and syntheses in knowledge/. Find papers by topic, method, author, keyword, or insight. Use when user asks "which papers did I read about X?", "search my notes for Y", "find papers mentioning Z", or "what do I know about W?".
4: argument-hint: [query]
5: allowed-tools: Bash(*), Read, Grep, Glob
6: ---
7: 
8: # Search Knowledge — Query Your Paper Reading History
9: 
10: Search: $ARGUMENTS
11: 
12: > **CRITICAL RULE: PDF IS BANNED.** This skill only reads from local knowledge notes.
13: > It does not download or access any external resources.
14: 
15: ## Constants
16: 
17: - **KNOWLEDGE_DIR** — `knowledge/` relative to project root. Contains `summary_*.md` notes.
18: - **DAILY_DIR** — `knowledge/daily/` relative to project root. Contains daily paper lists.
19: - **SYNTHESIS_DIR** — `knowledge/syntheses/` relative to project root. Contains synthesis outputs.
20: 
21: ## Workflow
22: 
23: ### Step 1: Parse Arguments
24: 
25: The `$ARGUMENTS` is a free-form search query. Parse it to identify:
26: 
27: - **Keywords**: Technical terms, method names, model names (e.g., "MCP", "RLHF", "attention")
28: - **Authors**: Person names (e.g., "Danqi Chen", "Ashish Vaswani")
29: - **Domains**: Research area names (e.g., "agent", "safety", "reasoning")
30: - **Concepts**: Broader ideas (e.g., "test-time compute", "tool use", "injection")
31: - **Modifiers** (optional):
32:   - `- tag: TAG` — only search notes with this tag in the filename
33:   - `- type: notes|syntheses|daily|all` — which knowledge sources to search (default: all)
34:   - `- verbose` — show full matching context instead of summaries
35: 
36: ### Step 2: Collect Knowledge Sources
37: 
38: Determine which sources to search based on `- type` modifier:
39: 
40: ```bash
41: # Notes (default: always)
42: find knowledge/ -name "summary_*.md" -not -path "knowledge/syntheses/*" | sort
43: 
44: # Syntheses
45: find knowledge/syntheses/ -name "*.md" | sort
46: 
47: # Daily lists
48: find knowledge/daily/ -name "*.md" | sort
49: ```
50: 
51: If `- tag: TAG` is specified, only include notes matching `summary_{TAG}.md`.
52: 
53: If no sources exist, report that the knowledge base is empty and suggest running `/daily-papers` and `/read-paper` first.
54: 
55: ### Step 3: Search Across All Sources
56: 
57: Use Grep to search for the query terms across all collected sources. Search with multiple strategies:
58: 
59: 1. **Exact keyword search**: Grep for the exact query terms
60: 2. **Related term search**: Grep for semantically related terms (e.g., "injection" → also search "adversarial", "attack", "security")
61: 3. **Author search**: If the query contains a name, search for it in author fields
62: 
63: For each match, capture:
64: - **Source file**: Which note/synthesis/daily list
65: - **Paper title**: From the note header
66: - **Match context**: The surrounding 2-3 lines showing the match
67: - **Section**: Which section of the note the match appears in (Problem, Method, Key Insights, Connections, etc.)
68: 
69: ### Step 4: Organize and Rank Results
70: 
71: Group results by relevance:
72: 
73: 1. **Direct matches** — query terms appear in title or core method description
74: 2. **Method matches** — query terms appear in Method or Key Equations sections
75: 3. **Insight matches** — query terms appear in Key Insights or Connections sections
76: 4. **Mention matches** — query terms appear in passing (Questions, Connections)
77: 
78: ### Step 5: Present Results
79: 
80: Present results in a structured format:
81: 
82: ```text
83: Found {N} matches for "{query}" across {M} papers
84: 
85: ## Papers where "{query}" is central
86: 
87: ### {Paper Title} ({arXiv ID})
88: - **Tag**: {tag}
89: - **Why relevant**: {one-line explanation of the match}
90: - **Key finding**: {the most relevant insight or method detail}
91: - **Note**: knowledge/summary_{tag}.md
92: 
93: ## Papers mentioning "{query}"
94: 
95: ### {Paper Title} ({arXiv ID})
96: - **Context**: {where and how it mentions the query}
97: - **Note**: knowledge/summary_{tag}.md
98: 
99: ## Synthesis insights
100: 
101: From synthesis_{date}.md:
102: - {relevant cross-paper finding or trend}
103: 
104: ## Daily paper matches
105: 
106: From {date}.md:
107: - {paper title} — {tier} — {why relevant}
108: ```
109: 
110: If `- verbose` is specified, include the full matching context for each result.
111: 
112: If no matches found, suggest:
113: - Alternative search terms
114: - Running `/daily-papers` to discover new papers on the topic
115: - Running `/read-paper` on specific papers to add them to the knowledge base
116: 
117: ### Step 6: Suggest Follow-ups
118: 
119: Based on search results, suggest:
120: - Papers that could be read next (`/read-paper ID`)
121: - Running `/synthesize - full` if the knowledge base has grown significantly
122: - Running `/daily-papers` to find more papers on underrepresented topics
123: 
124: ## Key Rules
125: 
126: - **PDF IS BANNED.** Never download or link to PDF files.
127: - **Search is local only.** Only search within `knowledge/` directory contents.
128: - **Be thorough.** Use multiple search strategies (exact, related, author) to avoid missing relevant papers.
129: - **Rank honestly.** Don't inflate relevance — distinguish between "central to the paper" and "mentioned in passing."
130: - **Preserve context.** Always show enough surrounding text for the user to understand why a match is relevant.
131: - **Report empty results clearly.** If nothing matches, say so and suggest alternatives rather than returning weak matches.

(End of file - total 131 lines)
</content>

1: ---
2: name: daily-papers
3: description: Fetch and filter daily LLM papers from arXiv. Only surfaces large language model related work — excludes CV, audio/video generation, robotics, and other non-LLM topics. Scores relevance, checks author quality, and tiers papers as MUST_READ / INTERESTING / MARGINAL / SKIP. Use when user says "daily papers", "today's papers", "what's new on arxiv", "find new LLM papers", or "check new papers".
4: argument-hint: [date-or-empty-for-today]
5: allowed-tools: Bash(*), Read, Write, WebFetch
6: ---
7: 
8: # Daily LLM Papers
9: 
10: Fetch, filter, and tier today's LLM papers from arXiv.
11: 
12: > **Scope**: Large language models only. CV, audio/video generation, robotics,
13: > medical imaging, wireless, recommender systems, and other non-LLM topics are
14: > automatically excluded.
15: 
16: ## Constants
17: 
18: - **CATEGORIES** — arXiv categories to scan: `cs.CL, cs.AI, cs.LG`
19: - **MAX_FETCH = 150** — Max papers to fetch per run
20: - **MIN_SCORE = 0.5** — Minimum relevance score to surface
21: - **FETCH_SCRIPT** — `tools/daily_papers.py` relative to project root
22: 
23: > Overrides (append to arguments):
24: > - `/daily-papers` — today's papers (default)
25: > - `/daily-papers 2026-04-10` — papers from a specific date
26: > - `/daily-papers - min-score: 2` — only high-relevance papers
27: > - `/daily-papers - check-authors: true` — look up first author on Semantic Scholar (slow)
28: > - `/daily-papers - categories: cs.CL` — only NLP category
29: > - `/daily-papers - max: 300` — fetch more papers
30: 
31: ## Workflow
32: 
33: ### Step 1: Parse Arguments
34: 
35: Parse `$ARGUMENTS` for:
36: 
37: - **Date**: `YYYY-MM-DD` format, or empty for today
38: - **`- min-score: N`**: override MIN_SCORE (default 0.5; use 2 for strict filtering)
39: - **`- check-authors: true`**: enable OpenAlex author quality lookup (free, no API key, ~0.3s per paper)
40: - **`- categories: CATS`**: override CATEGORIES
41: - **`- max: N`**: override MAX_FETCH
42: 
43: ### Step 2: Fetch and Filter
44: 
45: Run the fetch script:
46: 
47: ```bash
48: python3 tools/daily_papers.py run \
49:   --date "DATE" \
50:   --categories "CATEGORIES" \
51:   --max MAX_FETCH \
52:   --min-score MIN_SCORE \
53:   --check-authors  # only if requested
54: ```
55: 
56: If `tools/daily_papers.py` is not found, fall back to inline arXiv API search
57: with manual keyword filtering (less precise but works).
58: 
59: ### Step 3: Present Results
60: 
61: Present papers grouped by tier:
62: 
63: #### MUST_READ (score ≥ 4)
64: 
65: ```text
66: | # | arXiv ID | Title | Authors | Score | Author Quality |
67: |---|----------|-------|---------|-------|---------------|
68: ```
69: 
70: For each MUST_READ paper, also show:
71: - **Why relevant**: Which LLM keywords triggered the high score
72: - **First author**: Name + institution (if detected) + S2 profile (if checked)
73: - **Abstract**: Full abstract for quick assessment
74: 
75: #### INTERESTING (score 2–3.9)
76: 
77: Same table format, but only show first 2 lines of abstract.
78: 
79: #### MARGINAL (score 0.5–1.9)
80: 
81: Compact table only — title and score. No abstract.
82: 
83: ### Step 4: Author Quality Assessment
84: 
85: When `check-authors` is enabled (or for MUST_READ papers by default):
86: 
87: 1. **First author OpenAlex profile**: h-index, citation count, works count
88: 2. **Institution detection**: Match against known institution lists
89:    - **Tier 1**: OpenAI, DeepMind, Anthropic, Meta FAIR, Google Research/Brain, MS Research, top US/UK/CN universities
90:    - **Tier 2**: Strong regional universities, major tech companies (Alibaba, ByteDance, Tencent, Baidu, Huawei)
91:    - **Unknown**: No match found
92: 
93: 3. **Author quality labels**:
94:    - `high` — h-index ≥ 20 OR citations ≥ 5000 (established researcher)
95:    - `medium` — h-index ≥ 10 OR citations ≥ 1000 (productive researcher)
96:    - `low` — citations ≥ 100 but no strong track record (early career or potentially water papers)
97:    - `unknown` — OpenAlex lookup failed or no profile found
98: 
99: 4. **Water paper signals** (flag, don't auto-exclude):
100:    - First author with very low citation count AND from unknown institution
101:    - Title contains "survey" or "review" from unknown group
102:    - Paper is solely about applying existing method to a new domain without innovation
103:    - Multiple papers from same group with near-identical titles in short timeframe
104: 
105: ### Step 5: Save Summary
106: 
107: Save a dated summary to `knowledge/daily/`:
108: 
109: ```
110: knowledge/daily/YYYY-MM-DD.md
111: ```
112: 
113: Format:
114: 
115: ```markdown
116: # Daily LLM Papers — YYYY-MM-DD
117: 
118: ## MUST_READ (N)
119: 
120: ### [Title](arxiv_url)
121: - **Authors**: First Author et al. | **Institution**: [detected or Unknown] | **Quality**: [high/medium/low]
122: - **Score**: X.X | **Why**: [key LLM keywords that matched]
123: - **Abstract**: [full abstract]
124: 
125: ## INTERESTING (N)
126: 
127: | Paper | Score | Authors | Note |
128: |-------|-------|---------|------|
129: 
130: ## SKIPPED (N non-LLM papers filtered out)
131: 
132: Top skipped: [list 3-5 titles that were close to threshold]
133: ```
134: 
135: ### Step 6: Suggest Follow-up
136: 
137: For MUST_READ papers:
138: 
139: ```text
140: /arxiv "ARXIV_ID" - download    — download LaTeX source and read in detail
141: /research-lit "topic"            — broader literature context
142: ```
143: 
144: ## Relevance Scoring Logic
145: 
146: The scoring system uses weighted keyword matching:
147: 
148: **Positive signals (LLM relevance)**:
149: - **+2**: Core LLM keywords — "large language model", "LLM", "RLHF", "DPO", "chain-of-thought", "scaling law", "fine-tuning", specific model names (GPT-4, LLaMA, DeepSeek, etc.)
150: - **+1**: LLM-adjacent — "transformer", "MoE", "quantization", "RAG", "agent", "multimodal", "safety", "alignment", "KV cache", "language model"
151: - **+0.5**: Tangential — "NLP", "pretrain", "benchmark", "evaluation"
152: 
153: **Negative signals (non-LLM exclusion)**:
154: - **-3**: Strong exclude — "object detection", "video synthesis", "speech recognition", "medical imaging", "protein", "autonomous driving", "recommender system", "GAN"
155: - **-1**: Mild exclude — "diffusion model" (unless paired with LLM keywords), "image generation", "video generation"
156: 
157: **Tier thresholds**:
158: - `MUST_READ` ≥ 4: Directly about LLM methodology, training, or capabilities
159: - `INTERESTING` ≥ 2: LLM-related but may be application-focused or peripheral
160: - `MARGINAL` ≥ 0.5: Tangentially related, probably not core LLM work
161: - `SKIP` < 0.5: Not LLM-related
162: 
163: ## Key Rules
164: 
165: - **PDF IS BANNED.** Never suggest downloading PDFs. Only LaTeX source.
166: - Only surface LLM papers. If in doubt, exclude rather than include.
167: - Author quality is advisory, not deterministic — a paper from an unknown author
168:   can still be MUST_READ if the content is clearly important.
169: - Water paper flags are signals, not verdicts. Present them and let the user decide.
170: - Always show WHY a paper was scored the way it was (which keywords matched).
171: - For MUST_READ papers from top institutions, highlight the institution.
172: - The scoring system is conservative: better to miss a borderline paper than
173:   to flood the user with irrelevant work.
